key_questions: Mechanism of injury: inversion sprain (lateral ankle), fall, overuse?
Ability to weight-bear (Ottawa Rules: cannot bear weight × 4 steps → X-ray)?
Location of pain: bone tenderness (Ottawa Rules) vs ligament?
Ottawa Ankle Rules: bony tenderness at posterior tip/base of lateral malleolus or medial malleolus, or navicular, or base of 5th metatarsal → X-ray required
Timing since injury: acute (<48h) vs subacute/chronic?
red_flags: Ottawa Rules positive → X-ray to rule out fracture before treating as sprain
Significant swelling + inability to weight bear → possible fracture
Knee: inability to extend knee, joint effusion → ACL/meniscus tear — ortho
Recurrent 'sprain' in same location → ligament laxity, instability
ddx: Septic arthritis (hot, swollen joint + fever — emergency)

first_line.name: POLICE Protocol + Analgesia
first_line.drugs: Ibuprofen 400–600 mg q6–8h with food × 5–7 days | Naproxen 500 mg BID × 5–7 days | Acetaminophen 650–1000 mg q6h (if NSAID contraindicated)
first_line.notes: POLICE: Protection (support), Optimal Loading (early gentle movement), Ice, Compression, Elevation. Replace old RICE with POLICE — early controlled loading prevents stiffness. Ice 15–20 min q1–2h for first 48h. NSAIDs: reduce pain and swelling — superior to acetaminophen for acute musculoskeletal injury. Take with food.

first_line.name: Topical NSAID
first_line.drugs: Diclofenac gel 1% (Voltaren Emulgel) QID to affected area
first_line.notes: Topical diclofenac: equivalent efficacy to oral NSAIDs for local musculoskeletal pain with minimal systemic absorption. Preferred in elderly, renal disease, GI risk.

second_line.name: Compression + Splinting
second_line.drugs: Tensor bandage / elastic support | Air stirrup brace (ankle) for Grade II–III
second_line.notes: Compression reduces swelling. Ankle brace/splint: allows early ambulation while protecting ligaments. Grade III (complete tear): ortho referral.

non_pharm: Ice 15–20 min every 1–2 hours for first 48h (not directly on skin — towel wrap)
Elevation: keep injured limb above heart level
Early controlled movement (POLICE): begin gentle ROM exercises after 24–48h
Physiotherapy: for grade II–III sprains — significantly reduces re-injury risk
Return to activity progressively — pain is the guide
treatment.refer_when: Ottawa Rules positive (X-ray needed); complete ligament rupture (grade III); joint effusion; suspected fracture; significant functional impairment not improving in 2–3 weeks; recurrent instability
